quiz.title: Net Change Theorem Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Applications of the Net Change Theorem (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: The velocity of a particle moving along a line is given by \(v(t) = 3t^2 - 2t\) meters per second. Find the net change in position (displacement) from \(t = 1\) to \(t = 3\).
questions.explanation: Net change in position = \(\int_{1}^{3} v(t) dt = \int_{1}^{3} (3t^2 - 2t) dt = \left[ t^3 - t^2 \right]_{1}^{3} = (27 - 9) - (1 - 1) = 18 - 0 = 18\) meters.

questions.question: Water flows into a tank at a rate of \(r(t) = 4t + 2\) liters per minute. What is the net change in the volume of water from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 5\) minutes?
questions.explanation: Net change in volume = \(\int_{0}^{5} (4t + 2) dt = \left[ 2t^2 + 2t \right]_{0}^{5} = (50 + 10) - (0 + 0) = 60\) liters.

questions.question: The acceleration of a car is given by \(a(t) = 6t\) meters per second squared. If the car starts from rest, what is the net change in velocity from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 4\) seconds?
questions.explanation: Net change in velocity = \(\int_{0}^{4} 6t dt = \left[ 3t^2 \right]_{0}^{4} = 48 - 0 = 48\) meters per second.

questions.question: A population of bacteria grows at a rate of \(P'(t) = 500e^{0.1t}\) bacteria per hour. Find the net change in population from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 2\) hours.
The marginal cost of producing \(x\) units is given by \(C'(x) = 2x + 5\) dollars per unit. Find the net change in cost when production increases from 10 to 20 units.
The rate of change of temperature of a cooling object is \(\frac{dT}{dt} = -4e^{-0.5t}\) degrees Celsius per minute. Find the net change in temperature from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 3\) minutes.
questions.explanation: Net change = \(\int_{0}^{3} -4e^{-0.5t} dt = -4 \cdot \frac{1}{-0.5} \left[ e^{-0.5t} \right]_{0}^{3} = 8(e^{-1.5} - e^{0}) = 8(e^{-1.5} - 1)\) degrees Celsius. The negative value indicates a decrease in temperature.

questions.question: A particle moves along a line with velocity \(v(t) = \sin t\) meters per second. Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = \pi\).
questions.explanation: Net displacement = \(\int_{0}^{\pi} \sin t dt = \left[ -\cos t \right]_{0}^{\pi} = (-\cos \pi) - (-\cos 0) = (-(-1)) - (-1) = 1 + 1 = 2\) meters.

questions.question: The rate at which oil leaks from a tank is \(L'(t) = 2t\) gallons per hour. Find the net change in the amount of oil from \(t = 1\) to \(t = 4\) hours.
The derivative of a function \(f\) is given by \(f'(x) = \frac{1}{x}\) for \(x > 0\). Find the net change in \(f\) from \(x = 1\) to \(x = e\).
A car's velocity is given by \(v(t) = 20 - 2t\) meters per second. Find the net displacement from \(t = 0\) to \(t = 10\) seconds.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the Net Change Theorem with basic applications.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the Net Change Theorem well. Review the ones you missed.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up integrals from rate functions.
scoreMessages.0: Review the Net Change Theorem: \(\int_a^b F'(t) dt = F(b) - F(a)\).
